title: go-perf-agent hypothesis
description: One testable performance-optimization hypothesis grounded in a real telemetry signal.

file.description: source file (repo-relative) holding the edit site

line.description: approximate line of the edit site

evidence.description: the real-data signal that motivated this hypothesis

value.description: measured weight, e.g. 12.4% of CPU, 3.1GB alloc_space

query.description: exact gcx/pprof query that produced it

rationale.description: why this pattern applies to this symbol, tied to the evidence

metric.description: the benchstat metric expected to improve (the proof metric)

name.description: existing Benchmark name, or empty if one must be written

dependency.description: set when the fix lives in a dependency we don't own but can change (vendored OSS or generated code). It is a normal hypothesis; the harness requires the user to opt in (put path in scope) before validating, and shipping needs an upstream PR or vendor patch.

path.description: in-tree dir to edit, e.g. vendor/github.com/parquet-go/parquet-go

upstream.description: where to upstream the change, e.g. github.com/parquet-go/parquet-go

delta_pct.description: % change on the proof metric (negative = improvement)
